properties.description: Notebook that orchestrates ETL Processes defined in the ODW package

source:
`orchestration_transform_dependencies.yaml` in `odw-config` contains information about parameters and dependencies for each part of the ODW ETL Process. Each entry consists of an entity name, which would then contain a number of stages, with each stage containing kwargs to feed into `py_etl_executor` alongside dependent config entries that should be executed first. 

The aim of this notebook is to extract this orchestration config and generate an "execution order", which will be composed of a list of parameter groups. Each parameter group will then be executed sequentially, but the parameters within each group would be fed into `py_etl_executor` notebooks in parallel, which should maximise the throughput of the ETL pipeline

from odw.core.orchestration.dependency_resolver import DependencyResolver
from odw.core.etl.metadata_manager import MetadataManager
from odw.core.io.synapse_file_data_io import SynapseFileDataIO
from odw.core.util.logging_util import LoggingUtil
from odw.core.util.util import Util
from typing import List
import yaml
import json

### Parameters
- entity_stages_to_run: A list of the form `entity-name.stage` similar to the `depends_on` property in the `orchestration_transform_dependencies.yaml` file. If specified, then ONLY the elements of this list (and their transitive dependencies) will be returned. If not specified, then the full config will be returned
- `run_id`: The id of a previous execution to rerun from. If specified, then the execution history will be queried so that any parameters that have had successful runs for the associate run_id are skipped. If not specified, then it is assumed that all parameters should be kept
### Load the config file which contains the execution order for each entity/stage

config_data = SynapseFileDataIO().read(
    spark=spark,
    storage_endpoint=Util.get_storage_account(),
    container_name="odw-config",
    blob_path="orchestration/orchestration_transform_dependencies.yaml",
    file_format="text"
)
config_text = "\n".join([x.value for x in config_data.collect()])
config = yaml.safe_load(config_text)
### Load the execution history for the given run_id

execution_history = []
if run_id:
    # Todo need to query the execution history DB. This should be filled in as part of the work to add the execution history table
    execution_history = None
### Generate an execution order, which contains a list of entity-stages that can be executed in parallel, with redundant/irrelevant stages being skipped
### Return the execution order back to the pipeline
